revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 64x64 pixel-art terrain-tile sheet, no margins. Profile: snes16_platformer_v1. Preset: world_coastline_16. Asset kind: terrain-tile, asset type: tile. Exact sheet size 64x64px, arranged as 4 rows x 4 columns of 16x16px frames, 16 frames total.

Style: crisp 16-bit SNES-era pixel art, low-resolution readable pixels, no antialiasing blur, consistent palette, subtle one-pixel dark outline where useful, top-left warm sunlight. Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette: deep rich saturated greens like early-summer rice-field banks, clear blue-green water, vermilion and white accents only as tiny natural decorative hints if useful, soft warm sunlight. Not dry, not yellow-brown, not western prairie, not wasteland.

Subject: top-down transition tileset for a clear blue-green village pond meeting lush grassy banks. Calm rippling water with subtle sky reflection, mossy stones along shoreline, lush green bank edges, natural broken curved land-water boundaries. Think large readable world-map geography rather than close-up ground clutter. Avoid straight vertical or horizontal strips of water next to land; all shorelines should curve, chip, and break naturally. Water and banks must blend organically, not as hard rectangular bands.

STRICT TILE LAYOUT, each 16x16 cell filled edge-to-edge and seamlessly tileable:
Row 1: four full CENTER water variations, pure rippling clear blue-green pond water.
Row 2: edge transition tiles with neighboring grassy terrain on the North side, South side, West side, and East side respectively; curved bank-water boundaries, mossy stones, shallow-water edge highlights.
Row 3: outer corner transition tiles NW, NE, SW, SE respectively; rounded pond-corner shoreline shapes with grass banks and shallow water.
Row 4: four accent center water variations: water with small wave highlights, soft sky reflection, a few submerged mossy stones or tiny bright highlights, still mostly clear blue-green water.

Tilemap quality: internal frames may be 16x16 but final view must not read as a checkerboard. Vary texture, edge breakup, lighting, grass overlap, mossy stones, shallow water highlights, and subtle ripples while keeping cohesive identity and scale. Include usable center, edge, corner, chipped, grass-overlap, and decoration variants across the set. No visible grid lines, no borders, no gaps, no tile IDs, no labels, no text.

Containment: every frame stays inside its own 16x16 grid cell and keeps the same scale; no element crosses into another cell except seamless edge-to-edge tile continuity. All cells completely filled; use solid #FF00FF magenta only for any genuinely empty transparent-like leftover space, but since this is terrain tile art, the cells should be fully opaque terrain/water and no empty background should be visible.

Avoid absolutely: no Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, Sakuna characters, logos, likenesses, or iconography; no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature, no letter emblems, no modern text, no watermark, no UI.
